info.title: Release Management API - Build Distributions
info.description: **Looking for the old API?** You can find it here: V1 API spec The old API will be deprecated on February 1st 2027. Make sure that you have migrated to the new API before then. View the V1 → V2 Migration Guide

get.summary: Gets the list of the build distribution versions.
get.description: Gives back a list of build distributions versions.

parameters.name: app_id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the app the build distribution is connected to.

parameters.name: items_per_page
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of build distribution versions returned per request.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Specifies which page should be returned from the whole result set in a paginated scenario.

value.message: Unauthorized
Permission denied
Something went wrong

get.summary: Gets a list of test builds
get.description: Gives back a list of test builds for the given build distribution version.

parameters.name: app_id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the app the tester group is connected to.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: The version of the build distribution.

parameters.name: items_per_page
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of test builds returned per request.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Specifies which page should be returned from the whole result set in a paginated scenario.

value.message: Unauthorized
Permission denied
Something went wrong

get.summary: Gets a list of tester groups
get.description: Gives back a list of tester groups for the app. The `type` query parameter selects which groups are returned: `internal` (the default, Bitrise users) or `external` (email-only testers). For example, `?type=external` returns only the external tester groups.

parameters.name: app_id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the app the tester group is connected to.

parameters.name: items_per_page
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of tester groups returned per request.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Specifies which page should be returned from the whole result set in a paginated scenario.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Tester type. Defaults to internal.

parameters.name: installable_artifact_id
parameters.description: When provided, each tester group is decorated with `notification_sent` (whether a notification for this artifact has already been sent to the group) and, for external tester groups, `custom_access_link` (the access link sent to the testers: id, name, url, expires_at, access_code; omitted when there is none). The access code is not exposed via the public API.

value.message: Invalid type: `not-a-valid-type`. Valid values are: internal, external.
Unauthorized
Permission denied
Something went wrong

post.description: Creates a tester group for the app. Without a `type` (or with `type=internal`) an internal group of Bitrise users is created. With `type=external` an external group of email-only testers is created: provide a non-empty `emails` array (at most 1000 addresses, each at most 255 characters, syntactically validated) and a tester is created for every address and added to the group. The `emails` field is ignored for internal groups. For example, create an external group with `?type=external` and body `{ "app_id": "<uuid>", "name": "My external testers", "emails": ["tester1@example.com", "tester2@example.com"] }`.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Tester type. Defaults to internal.

value.message: param is missing or the value is empty or invalid: name
At least one email is required for an external tester group.
Some of the provided emails are invalid.
Invalid type: `not-a-valid-type`. Valid values are: internal, external.

422.description: Tester group name has already taken.

value.message: Validation failed: Name has already been taken
Unauthorized
You have no permissions to create tester group. Only Admins, Owners and Workspace Admins can create new tester groups.
Something went wrong

app_id.description: An uuidV4 identifier for your app.

name.description: The name of the new tester group. Should be unique in the scope of the app.

auto_notify.description: If set to true it indicates the tester group will receive notifications automatically.

emails.description: Email addresses added as external testers to the group. Required for external tester groups (at least one, at most 1000, each at most 255 characters). Ignored for internal tester groups.

delete.description: Deletes a tester group. For external tester groups, any external tester that is left without membership in any group is also removed; internal testers are always kept. The `type` query parameter must match the group's type.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the tester group.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Tester type. Defaults to internal.

value.message: Invalid type: `not-a-valid-type`. Valid values are: internal, external.
The resource is of type `internal` and cannot be accessed with type `external`.
Unauthorized
You have no permissions to destroy the tester group. Only Admins, Owners and Workspace Admins can destroy tester groups.
Something went wrong

get.description: Gives back the details of the selected tester group. The `type` query parameter must match the group's type (`internal` by default, or `external`); for example, use `?type=external` to fetch an external group. A non-matching type returns a validation error.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the tester group.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Tester type. Defaults to internal.

value.message: Invalid type: `not-a-valid-type`. Valid values are: internal, external.
The resource is of type `internal` and cannot be accessed with type `external`.
Unauthorized
Permission denied
Something went wrong

put.description: Updates the given tester group. Internal tester groups can update their `name` and `auto_notify`. External tester groups have no `auto_notify`, so for them this endpoint only renames the group (any `auto_notify` value sent is ignored). The `type` query parameter must match the group's type.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the tester group.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Tester type. Defaults to internal.

value.message: Validation failed: Name has already been taken
Invalid type: `not-a-valid-type`. Valid values are: internal, external.
The resource is of type `internal` and cannot be accessed with type `external`.
Unauthorized
You have no permissions to update the tester group. Only Admins, Owners and Workspace Admins can update tester groups.
Something went wrong

name.description: The new name of the tester group. Should be unique in the scope of the app.

auto_notify.description: If set to true it indicates the tester group will receive notifications automatically.

post.summary: Add testers to a tester group
post.description: Adds testers to a tester group for the app. For internal groups provide `user_slugs` (Bitrise users); for external groups (`type=external`) provide an `emails` array (at most 1000) and a tester is created/reused for each address. The array that does not match the group type is ignored.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the tester group.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Tester type. Defaults to internal.

value.message: param is missing or the value is empty or invalid: user_slugs
Some of the provided emails are invalid.
Invalid type: `not-a-valid-type`. Valid values are: internal, external.
The resource is of type `internal` and cannot be accessed with type `external`.
Unauthorized
You have no permissions to add testers to the tester group. Only Admins, Owners and Workspace Admins can add new testers to the tester groups.
Something went wrong

user_slugs.description: User slugs to add as internal testers. Required for internal tester groups; ignored for external groups.

emails.description: Email addresses to add as external testers. Required for external tester groups (at most 1000); ignored for internal groups.

post.description: Notify an internal tester group about a new test build. To notify external tester groups, use the custom access link creation endpoint with tester_group_id.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the tester group.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Tester type. Defaults to internal.

value.message: External tester groups cannot be notified via this endpoint. Create a custom access link with tester_group_id to notify them.
param is missing or the value is empty or invalid: test_build_id
The provided artifact cannot be used for tester group notifications, id: `e8469a17-5fe8-4d59-abad-52fc8a94e729`
The provided artifact cannot be used for tester group notifications, id: `b6690148-4e1f-4d66-a554-d25c24397a42`
The provided artifact cannot be used for tester group notifications, id: `dde4bfd4-c9c3-4fb8-bb1c-85b34aaf8da9`
Invalid type: `not-a-valid-type`. Valid values are: internal, external.
The resource is of type `internal` and cannot be accessed with type `external`.
Unauthorized
You have no permissions to notify the tester group. Only Admins, Owners and Workspace Admins can send notifications to the tester groups.
Something went wrong

test_build_id.description: The unique identifier of the test build what will be sent in the notification of the tester group.

get.summary: Gets a list of potential tester candidates to the tester groups
get.description: Gives back a paginated list of tester candidates.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the tester group.

parameters.name: items_per_page
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of potential testers that this route will return for a page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Specifies which page should be returned from the whole result set in a paginated scenario.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Searches for potential testers based on email or username, using a case-insensitive starts with search.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Tester type. Defaults to internal.

value.message: Invalid type: `not-a-valid-type`. Valid values are: internal, external.
The resource is of type `internal` and cannot be accessed with type `external`.
Unauthorized
You have no permissions to list the potential testers. Only Admins, Owners and Workspace Admins can list the potential testers.
Something went wrong

get.description: Gives back a list of testers.

parameters.name: app_id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the app the tester group is connected to.

parameters.name: tester_group_id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of a tester group.

parameters.name: items_per_page
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of testers returned per request.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Specifies which page should be returned from the whole result set in a paginated scenario.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Tester type. Defaults to internal.

value.message: Invalid type: `not-a-valid-type`. Valid values are: internal, external.
Unauthorized
Permission denied
Something went wrong

delete.description: Deletes a tester. This means that the endpoint will delete the specified tester from all tester groups in Build Distribution. In case you only want to delete the tester from a specific tester group use the optional tester_group_id parameter.

parameters.name: app_id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the app the tester group is connected to.

parameters.name: tester_slug
parameters.description: The slug of the tester.

parameters.name: tester_group_id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of a tester group. In case it is given, the specified tester will only be deleted from this specific tester group.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Tester type. Defaults to internal.

value.message: Invalid type: `not-a-valid-type`. Valid values are: internal, external.
The resource is of type `internal` and cannot be accessed with type `external`.
Unauthorized
Permission denied
Something went wrong

baseURL.description: The server base URL with the scheme.

token.description: PAT necessary to use API calls
token.name: authorization
